page: Microsoft Azure E64s_v7 is an Esv7-family virtual machine configured with 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-per-core ratio. Powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz, this x86_64 architecture server features 32 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The processor includes a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache per core, and 48 KB L1 instruction cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and integer operations. This instance does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

Benchmarks indicate strong multi-core capabilities and memory throughput. The server achieved a multi-core stress-ng CPU score of 63502.15 and a PassMark CPU score of 80219.92, placing it in the top tier of tested hardware. Memory performance is highlighted by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 4689.08 GB/sec and uncached large block read bandwidth of 329.32 GB/sec. Database operations are highly efficient, with PassMark database operations scoring 29091.56 and Redis SET operations reaching 105.13 million operations per second. Single-core performance is moderate, with a stress-ng single-core score of 2206.19.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E64s_v7 offers a high-density memory configuration relative to its compute resources, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads without the overhead of dedicated GPU hardware. The absence of local storage requires reliance on external Premium Storage, which should be factored into architectural planning. This server is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, enterprise database hosting, Redis caching layers, large-scale data processing, and workloads utilizing Intel AMX instructions for CPU-based inference.
description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v7 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the Esv7 family, featuring 64 vCPUs and 512.0 GB of RAM, which provides an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio. It runs on a dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor with 32 physical cores clocked at 4.2 GHz, utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The processor is equipped with a 480 MB L3 cache and supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks show strong multi-core performance, with a PassMark CPU score of 80219.92, and exceptional memory bandwidth, reaching 329.32 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. It also demonstrates high database efficiency, processing 105.13 million Redis SET operations per second. This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, including enterprise databases, high-throughput Redis caching, and large-scale data processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v7 is an Esv7-family virtual machine featuring 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon processor. It is optimized for memory-intensive databases and high-throughput enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E64s_v7 is a memory-optimized VM with 64 vCPUs, 512 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for enterprise databases and caching.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and advanced matrix extensions for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 64 vCPUs and 512 GB RAM providing an 8.0 GB memory-to-core ratio
Dedicated Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor clocked at 4.2 GHz
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction set support for accelerated vector math
Strong multi-core performance with 80219.92 PassMark CPU score
High-throughput memory bandwidth and 105.13 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for enterprise databases, Redis caching, and memory-bound data processing
